rulings.Case Title: EMELIE B SANTANA VS. BANK OF AMERICA HOME LOANS et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, August 9, 2013, line 2.- DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Emelie Santana v. Bank of America Home Loans. Sustained without leave to amend as to the Second, Third, Fourth, Fifth, Seventh, Eighth, Ninth, Tenth, Eleventh, Twelfth, Thirteenth, Fourteenth, Fifteenth, and Sixteen Causes of Action. After the demurrer to the First Amended Complaint was sustained, Plaintiff was given final leave to amend to allege facts showing tolling of statutes of limitation; to allege tender; to allege duty; to allege terms of contracts allegedly breached and standing; fraud with particularity differentiating between defendants. Plaintiff failed to do so. As to the fourth cause of action, Plaintiff failed to allege standing regarding and tolling of statute of limitations and leave to amend is unlikely to cure this defect. The demurrer is overruled as to the Sixth Cause of Action because it does not name a demurrering Defendant. =(D501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: D. BUCKLEY PROPERTIES INC., et al VS. PALOMA GAOS et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, August 9, 2013, line 3. - DEFENDANTS PALOMA GAOS AND ANDRES LOPEZS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the First, Third, and Fourth Causes of Action. The complaint is not conclusory and alleges concrete facts. That the funds have already been returned is a factual issue not to be decided on demurrer. It serves no useful purpose to sustain with leave to amend the demurrer as to the conspiracy cause of action. It cannot be said, as a matter of law, that Plaintiff does not have a privacy interest in his mailbox and home. The demurrer is off calendar as to the Second Cause of Action because it was dismissed by Plaintiff on July 29, 2013. = (D501/REQ)
